commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat(cli): add generate command for text/image/video/tts/asr (#12799)

* ✨ feat(cli): add generate command for text/image/video/tts/asr

LOBE-5711

- `lh generate text <prompt>` — LLM text completion with SSE streaming
  - Supports --model (provider/model format), --system, --temperature, --pipe
- `lh generate image <prompt>` — Image generation via async task
- `lh generate video <prompt>` — Video generation via async task
- `lh generate tts <text>` — Text-to-speech (openai/microsoft/edge backends)
- `lh generate asr <file>` — Speech-to-text via OpenAI Whisper
- `lh generate status` — Check async generation task status
- `lh generate list` — List generation topics
- Add shared HTTP auth helper (api/http.ts) for webapi endpoints

commit.message:
Fix IDE lock file race condition in wt-agent-launch

The hide/restore mechanism for isolating neovim lock files broke
concurrent sessions — hidden locks from one agent launch would
block other sessions from connecting to their neovim instances.

Remove the hide_other_locks/restore_hidden_locks machinery and let
Claude handle CWD-based IDE matching natively via --ide flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Resolve open question #22: Counterspell timing in async play

Spell name revealed but cast level hidden. Player picks slot via Discord
buttons. If slot < cast level, player rolls spellcasting ability check
(DC 10 + spell level) — no auto-roll. Enemy turn continues async;
success retroactively removes spell effects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: DnDnD
repository.description: DnD in Discord!

commit.message:
Add server-side explicit refusal detection for short dismissals

'싫어', '패스', 'skip' etc. were not server-side detected, causing the AI
to misclassify them as normal answers and repeat the same question.
detectExplicitRefusal() catches these and forces reason:refused → proceed:true,
so the AI shows an impact note and moves to the next step instead of looping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add exponential backoff and dedup to Telegram bridge polling (#631) (#762)

Hardens the Telegram bridge against poll failures and duplicate update
processing. On error, retries with exponential backoff (500ms base,
30s cap) instead of fixed 500ms. Uses DedupService to skip already-
processed update_ids, preventing re-execution after error recovery.

Addresses Telegram sub-tasks in #631 (bridge hardening).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(claude-engineer): add security engineer agent profile and manager job (#103)

## Summary

- Add `security-engineer` persistent engineer agent profile with focus
areas tailored to real-world risks: command/script injection (including
GitHub Actions expression injection), secrets exposure, unsafe shell
patterns, supply chain risks (action SHA pinning), permissions
over-scoping, input validation gaps, and sensitive data in logs
- Add `security-engineer` manager job to `repo-engineer-managers.yml` to
run daily alongside docs-engineer and code-janitor
- Rewrite focus areas and scanning strategy from original generic
web-app checklist to be practical and technology-appropriate for repos
using GitHub Actions and shell scripts

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `repo-engineer-managers.yml` is valid YAML and the new job
follows the same pattern as existing jobs
- [ ] Verify `security-engineer.md` follows the same structure as
`docs-engineer.md` and `code-janitor.md`
- [ ] Manually trigger workflow dispatch to confirm the security
engineer job runs

commit.message:
Fix drive selection blocking UI by moving path resolution to background thread

resolve_drive_path had blocking exists() calls that froze the UI before
the async navigate_to could show the spinner. Now drive path resolution
runs entirely in the background via navigate_to_with_resolver, showing
a placeholder path with spinner immediately.

commit.message:
VIPTrack v0.0.6 - Bug fixes from audit

Bug 1: URL filter param not clamped to valid values
- Shared links with ?filter=commercial would show empty map
- Now only accepts mil-vip, military, government, vip

Bug 2: MiniMap missing all 11 new map styles
- MiniMap fell back to dark for any new style
- Added all 17 tile URLs to miniMap styleUrls

Bug 3: PIA (Privacy ICAO Address) aircraft excluded
- PIA aircraft use anonymized hex codes, often government/VIP
- Now included in the data processing gate alongside mil/vip

commit.message:
Smooth breathing motion, deeper blue, slow fading rings

Surface: Removed beat-based seed jumping entirely. Motion is now
continuous slow time drift (seed = t * 0.04) with very slow amplitude
tracking (α=0.03) and slow height interpolation (α=0.04). The surface
breathes gently with the music instead of jumping around.

Colors: Much deeper blue — valleys are dark navy, peaks are icy
blue-white. All scene lights tinted blue.

Rings: Removed ripples entirely. Rings now have slow breathing opacity
(sine wave at 0.4 Hz) modulated by audio energy. Very slow orbit,
blue-tinted glow. Purely complementary accent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive 3-node network and learning verification tests

- test_3node_network.py: Simulates 3 BAZINGA nodes joining network
  - Peer discovery (multicast/gossip simulation)
  - Triadic PoB consensus (3 nodes generate proofs)
  - Blockchain sync (knowledge attestation + mining)
  - Federated gradient sharing (LoRA + phi-weighted aggregation)
  - DAO governance (tau-weighted voting with 61.8% threshold)
  - Full round trip (discover -> train -> share -> mine -> sync)

- test_actual_learning.py: Proves BAZINGA actually learns
  - LoRA weights change during training
  - Loss decreases over iterations
  - Gradients are non-zero
  - Federated aggregation combines knowledge from multiple nodes
  - Model output changes after training
  - End-to-end federated training converges

All 36 tests passing (29 Darmiyan + 7 Learning).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
